lede: A look at November 24 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Carmelita Jeter and Maria Höfl-Riesch, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Hibiscus Rising
events.desc: Hibiscus Rising, commemorating David Oluwale, is unveiled in Leeds.

events.title: Colombia
events.desc: The government of Colombia and the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia–People's Army sign a revised peace deal, bringing an end to the country's more than 50-year-long civil war.

events.title: Iran
events.desc: Iran signs an interim agreement with the P5+1 countries, limiting its nuclear program in exchange for reduced sanctions.

events.title: Avdhela Project
events.desc: The Avdhela Project, an Aromanian digital library and cultural initiative, is founded in Bucharest, Romania.

events.title: 1976 Çaldıran–Muradiye earthquake
events.desc: The Çaldıran–Muradiye earthquake in eastern Turkey kills between 4,000 and 5,000 people.

events.title: Washington (state)
events.desc: During a severe thunderstorm over Washington state, a hijacker calling himself Dan Cooper (aka D. B. Cooper) parachutes from a Northwest Orient Airlines plane with $200,000 in ransom money. He has never been found.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The First Slovak Republic becomes a signatory to the Tripartite Pact, officially joining the Axis powers.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Battle of Lookout Mountain: Near Chattanooga, Tennessee, Union forces under General Ulysses S. Grant capture Lookout Mountain and begin to break the Confederate siege of the city led by General Braxton Bragg.

events.title: Charles Darwin
events.desc: British naturalist Charles Darwin's On the Origin of Species is published.

events.title: Genghis Khan
events.desc: Genghis Khan defeats the renegade Khwarazmian prince Jalal al-Din at the Battle of the Indus, completing the Mongol conquest of Central Asia.

births.name: Zachary Taylor
births.line: American general and politician, 12th President of the United States

births.name: Stephen Merchant
births.line: English actor, director, producer, and screenwriter

deaths.text: Freddie Mercury, Tanzanian-English singer-songwriter, lead vocalist of Queen, and producer
Lee Harvey Oswald, American assassin of John F. Kennedy
Robert Cecil, Viscount Cecil of Chelwood, 1st Viscount Cecil of Chelwood, English lawyer and politician, Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, Nobel Prize laureate
Ulrika Eleonora of Sweden, Queen of Sweden
Leszek I the White, High Duke of Poland (born c. 1186)

observances.text: Christian feast days: Andrew Dũng-Lạc, Pierre Dumoulin-Borie, and other Vietnamese Martyrs
